draft.introduction: Surah Al-Ahzab (The Confederates) addresses events of the Medinan period, including the Battle of the Trench and regulations for the Prophet’s household. It contains key verses on the prophetic model (uswa hasana) and the “trust” offered to creation.

translation.text: There has certainly been for you in the Messenger of God an excellent pattern for anyone whose hope is in God and the Last Day and who remembers God often. Indeed, We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, and they declined to bear it and feared it; but man undertook it. Indeed, he was unjust and ignorant.
translation.original_text: لَقَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ

translator_notes.note: The “trust” (amana) offered to and refused by creation but accepted by humanity is one of the Quran’s most mysterious verses. Interpreters have identified it with free will, moral responsibility, or the covenant with God. Humanity’s acceptance is both its glory and its peril.
